text.title: Migration Execution, Cutover, and Validation
text.summary: Three-run migration strategy, freeze period, post-cutover validation, parallel-run period, and rollback requirements.

rule.statement: Run the migration three times: dev-environment test, full dry-run with rollback on a clone of production, real cutover; each run catches a different class of bug.
rule.rationale: Dev test catches logic errors. Full dry-run catches rate limits, size limits, and data-edge cases that only appear at scale. Real cutover catches timing and concurrency issues under live load.

list.item: Run 1 (dev test): validate field mappings, auth, and basic ETL flow on a small data sample.
Run 2 (dry-run): run against a full production data clone; validate counts, comments, and attachments; confirm rollback path.
Run 3 (real cutover): freeze source, execute migration, validate three numbers, declare success or rollback.

rule.statement: Freeze the source tool 4 hours before cutover; route new tickets created during the window to a known "intake" queue and re-key them by hand after cutover.
rule.rationale: Issues created during migration create reconciliation complexity. A short freeze with a manual intake queue is simpler and less risky than attempting live-sync.

rule.statement: Validate three numbers before declaring migration successful: total issue count, total comment count, and total attachment volume by byte.
rule.rationale: Three independent counts at different granularities catch distinct failure modes: issues lost, comments orphaned, and attachments silently dropped at size limits.

rule.statement: Manually sample-validate 50 random issues by hand, comparing source to target field-by-field; automated count validation does not catch field-content corruption.
rule.rationale: Status mapping bugs, Markdown-to-HTML conversion failures, and @mention resolution failures appear in field content, not in counts.

example.note: After migrating 12,000 Jira issues to Linear: count matched (12,000 = 12,000); comment count matched (47,831 = 47,831); attachment bytes matched (8.4 GB = 8.4 GB). Manual sample of 50 issues revealed 3 with status mapping errors (Resolved → Done instead of In Review). Fix applied; re-run of status correction script; re-validate.

rule.statement: Keep the source tool read-only for at least 30 days after cutover; users will need to look up historical context for months after the switch.
rule.rationale: Post-cutover lookup requests are predictable and frequent. Read-only access at no cost is safer than full decommission and cheaper than rebuilding access when needed.

antipattern.description: Decommissioning the source tool immediately after cutover success.
antipattern.reason: "Inevitably someone needs to look up what was decided in PROJ-456" — this is guaranteed to happen within days. Premature decommission forces re-provisioning under pressure.

text.reference: Source vs target count comparison with 1% drift threshold
Field mapping table with "won't migrate" documentation column
